3.q: Overexpression of AhUGT75A resulted in accumulating less amount of malondialdehyde (MDA) and superoxide... AhUGT75A played important roles in conferring abiotic stress tolerance through reactive oxygen species scavenging.

4.q: Silencing AhGST23 disrupted glutathione-related metabolism in peanut. This disruption was manifested by reduced contents of ascorbic acid (AsA), dehydroascorbate (DHA), glutathione (GSH), and oxidized glutathione (GSSG)

6.q: AhMYB30 may increase salt and freezing stress tolerance as the transcription factor (TF) in Arabidopsis through both DREB/CBF and ABA-signaling pathways.

8.q: the overexpression of AhSPL5 can enhance salt tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis by enhancing its ROS-scavenging capability and positively regulating the expression of stress-responsive genes.

9.q: These results indicate that AhNCED4-overexpressing plants have an enhanced ROS-scavenging capacity.

10.q: The AhCytb6 gene protects the plant from ROS buildup during stress conditions... transgenic lines showed significantly lower production of MDA and H2O2 compared to WT and VC plants.

11.q: silencing AhERF28 enhanced the tolerance of peanut to salt and drought stress, likely due to an increased capacity of ROS scavenging.

12.q: overexpressing AhCDPK in tobacco could alleviate PSII photoinhibition by improving physiological states, such as reducing the accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS), improving the activity of antioxidant defence system enzymes

13.q: Trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase (TPP) is a pivotal enzyme in trehalose biosynthesis which plays an essential role in plant development and in the abiotic stress response.

14.q: Trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase (TPP) is a pivotal enzyme in trehalose biosynthesis which plays an essential role in plant development and in the abiotic stress response.

15.q: Cytb6 is a key regulatory unit of the electron transport chain in plants and affects the photosynthetic efficiency and yield of plants; transgenic plants maintained high photosynthesis under stress conditions.

16.q: several AhHsf genes (AhHsf5, AhHsf11, AhHsf20, AhHsf24, AhHsf30, AhHsf35) were induced by drought and salt stresses. Furthermore, the stress-induced member AhHsf20 was found to be located in nucleus.

18.q: CSDs are important antioxidant enzymes that prevent the accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) from damaging cells by the dismutation of superoxide free radical to hydrogen peroxide

20.q: Electrophoretic mobility shift assays (EMSA) showed that ABI4 was able to bind to the promoters of HSP70, fructokinase (FRK), and pyruvate kinase (PK) coding genes in vitro.

21.q: Electrophoretic mobility shift assays (EMSA) showed that ABI4 was able to bind to the promoters of HSP70, fructokinase (FRK), and pyruvate kinase (PK) coding genes in vitro.

22.q: Electrophoretic mobility shift assays (EMSA) showed that ABI4 was able to bind to the promoters of HSP70, fructokinase (FRK), and pyruvate kinase (PK) coding genes in vitro.

23.q: The TR banana plants conferred tolerance against the abiotic stresses by maintaining a high redox state of ascorbate and glutathione, which correlated with lower accumulation of H2O2, O2⋅− and higher level of antioxidant enzyme (SOD, APX, CAT, GR, DHAR and MDHAR) activities.

24.q: transgenic Arabidopsis overexpressing AhNAC2 lines were hypersensitive to ABA in root growth, seed germination, and stomatal closure compared to wild type Arabidopsis.

25.q: ectopic expression of AhcAPX gene in banana enhances the tolerance to drought and salt stress by annulling the damage caused by ROS

26.q: AhPEPC3 and AhPEPC4 are correlated with each other, indicating comparative associations with roots... the correlation of AhPEPC4 and AhPEPC3 implies that they may have similar functions.

27.q: The key gene families involved in the glutathione metabolism (AhGSTs, AhGPXs) and AsA-GSH cycle (AhGRs, AhAPXs, and AhMDHARs) were highlighted as central players in the antioxidant system.

28.q: The key gene families involved in the glutathione metabolism (AhGSTs, AhGPXs) and AsA-GSH cycle (AhGRs, AhAPXs, and AhMDHARs) were highlighted as central players in the antioxidant system.

29.q: The key gene families involved in the glutathione metabolism (AhGSTs, AhGPXs) and AsA-GSH cycle (AhGRs, AhAPXs, and AhMDHARs) were highlighted as central players in the antioxidant system.

30.q: The key gene families involved in the glutathione metabolism (AhGSTs, AhGPXs) and AsA-GSH cycle (AhGRs, AhAPXs, and AhMDHARs) were highlighted as central players in the antioxidant system.

31.q: In the pathway of polyamines synthesis, S-adenosylmethionine decarboxylase (SAMDC) is one of the key enzymes.

32.q: Groundnut AhcAPX conferred abiotic stress tolerance in transgenic banana through modulation of the ascorbate–glutathione pathway

33.q: The superoxide dismutase is the first enzyme involved in the detoxification of ROS and converts superoxide (O2-) radicals to H2O2.

34.q: These results indicate that AhNAC2 is a major player in the NAC gene family involved in ABA signaling.

35.q: isoform AhCSD1-2.2 lacks a miR398 binding site (Fig. 1c, red box)... transcript levels of three AhCSD1 isoforms under the miR398 co-infiltration were measured by qRT-PCR
